title: The function and failure of sensory predictions.

assets.abstract: Humans and other primates are equipped with neural mechanisms that allow them to automatically make predictions about future events, facilitating processing of expected sensations and actions. Prediction-driven control and monitoring of perceptual and motor acts are vital to normal cognitive functioning. This review provides an overview of corollary discharge mechanisms involved in predictions across sensory modalities and discusses consequences of predictive coding for cognition and behavior. C

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
